content: WhatsApp Web DOM scraping is inherently fragile: extensions rely on selectors like .message-in/.message-out/.selectable-text which break on WhatsApp UI updates. WhatsApp uses obfuscated/hashed class names that change with releases. Mitigations: prefer data-* attributes and structural/semantic anchors over class names, centralize all selectors in one adapter module, add runtime self-tests that detect selector failure and degrade gracefully with user-facing 'update needed' messaging, monitor breakage via telemetry. Competing approaches use internal WhatsApp module store (wppconnect/wa-js) which is more powerful but deeper into ToS violation territory.
